business_description.what_it_does: MYR Group is a specialty electrical construction services company operating through Transmission and Distribution (T&D) and Commercial and Industrial (C&I) segments in the United States and Canada.
relevant_business_lines: T&D services for transmission/distribution networks, substations, clean energy projects, EV charging infrastructure, construction, upgrade, maintenance and repair
C&I electrical contracting, including commercial/industrial wiring and typical C&I contracts that include data centers
less_relevant_or_not_proven: MYRG is not a data-center pure play; reviewed filings do not disclose data-center-specific revenue, margin, backlog, named customers, or contract values.

role_in_sector_value_chain.segment: Electrical contracting, substation, and grid interconnection services
role_in_sector_value_chain.role: Enables data center power delivery by providing electrical construction, substation/distribution work, commercial wiring, maintenance, and repair services.

current_sector_ranking.segment_name: Electrical contracting, substation, and grid interconnection services
current_sector_ranking.one_sentence_thesis: MYRG is a focused electrical contractor with utility T&D and C&I/data-center adjacency, positive FCF, and less extreme sales multiples than the most crowded equipment names.

Market data: market cap $6.94B, EV/Sales 1.788, FCF $227.8M.
what_to_verify_next: margin durability in C&I and T&D

chokepoint_thesis.potential_chokepoint: Qualified electrical contracting and interconnection execution capacity may become a bottleneck as AI data centers increase power demand and require substation, distribution, and C&I electrical work.
proven: MYRG performs T&D and C&I electrical construction services.
MYRG identifies data centers as a typical C&I contract category.
MYRG reports large backlog/RPO and comments that AI/electrification-driven electricity demand should require customer investment.
inferred: MYRG may benefit from AI data center power infrastructure demand if data-center projects become a larger share of C&I/T&D work.
Contractor capacity may be scarce in power-constrained regions.
not_proven: MYRG-specific AI data center contract wins, backlog dollars, customer names, or segment margins.

hard_evidence.summary: MYRG performs construction services through T&D and C&I segments. T&D covers electric transmission/distribution networks, substations, clean energy projects, and EV charging infrastructure; C&I covers design, installation, maintenance, and repair of commercial and industrial wiring.

MYRG states typical C&I contracts cover electrical contracting services for data centers, clean energy projects, airports, hospitals, hotels, commercial/industrial facilities, manufacturing plants, and other facilities.
MYRG says increasing electricity demand from electrification, new technologies including AI, and reshoring will require significant investment by customers in both reporting segments.
MYRG reports backlog of $2.84B at March 31, 2026, including T&D backlog of $980.7M and C&I backlog of $1.86B. Remaining performance obligations were $2.53B, with $2.22B expected within 12 months.

For 2025, MYRG reported T&D revenue of $2.00B, or 54.7% of revenue, and C&I revenue of $1.66B, or 45.3% of revenue. C&I revenue increased $173.6M year over year, and total revenue increased 8.8% to $3.66B.
Q1 2026 revenue increased 20.0% to $1.00B, driven by C&I revenue growth of $87.6M and T&D revenue growth of $79.2M. Gross margin increased to 13.4% from 11.6%.
Market data shows market cap $6.94B, enterprise value $6.84B, EV/Sales 1.788, P/S 1.8146299, EV/EBITDA 26.171, free cash flow $227.8M, and net debt $-101.7M.

soft_evidence.summary: MYRG says bidding and construction activity for small-to-medium transmission projects and upgrades remains active, and large multi-year project awards in 2026 would likely affect results more in 2027 or later. This supports timing optionality but does not prove specific data-center backlog.

MYRG describes long-standing customer relationships, a skilled workforce, centralized fleet, safety performance, and ability to take on additional and more complex projects. These are relevant to contractor qualification but do not prove data-center customer wins.

inference.inference: MYRG is more likely an enabling infrastructure contractor than a proprietary product chokepoint. Its chokepoint potential comes from scarce electrical contracting execution capacity, substation/interconnection capabilities, and project qualification rather than unique equipment IP.

MYRG may be a cleaner first deep-dive target than POWL/MOD because its sales multiples are lower and market data shows positive FCF/net cash, but high price momentum means it is not underfollowed enough for High Priority without more contract/customer evidence.

missing_evidence: Data-center-specific backlog is not disclosed; total backlog and C&I backlog are available, but data-center backlog is missing.
Named data-center customers, contracts, awards, and project values are not disclosed in the reviewed evidence.
Segment revenue is disclosed only for T&D and C&I; revenue specifically from data centers or AI power infrastructure is missing.
Segment margins for data-center work are not disclosed.
Customer concentration by data-center/hyperscaler/general contractor exposure is missing.
Ownership, short interest, analyst coverage, and peer/historical valuation context are missing from this package.
Future optionality around AI-driven electrical demand is not current commercial validation unless tied to contracts, backlog, or revenue.

valuation_and_crowding.summary: MYRG has populated market data: market cap $6.94B, EV $6.84B, EV/Sales 1.788, P/S 1.8146299, EV/EBITDA 26.171, FCF $227.8M, net debt $-101.7M. Crowding risk is elevated because 6m price change is 102.1%, 12m price change is 172.8%, and the stock is 8.0% below its 52-week high.
valuation_and_crowding.valuation_assessment: Reasonable on sales multiples relative to crowded equipment names, but not obviously cheap after strong price performance and elevated P/E/EV-to-EBITDA.

cash_flow_debt_dilution_balance_sheet.assessment: Market data shows positive free cash flow and net cash. This supports Watchlist status, but it does not answer data-center-specific commercial validation.

customer_backlog_contract_segment_revenu.backlog: Hard evidence: total backlog $2.84B at March 31, 2026; T&D $980.7M and C&I $1.86B. Data-center-specific backlog is missing.
customer_backlog_contract_segment_revenu.remaining_performance_obligations: Hard evidence: $2.53B RPO at March 31, 2026; $2.22B expected within 12 months.
customer_backlog_contract_segment_revenu.contracts: MYRG describes written awards, letters of intent, notices to proceed, and work orders as backlog criteria, but named data-center contracts/awards are missing.
customer_backlog_contract_segment_revenu.segment_revenue: Hard evidence: 2025 T&D revenue $2.00B and C&I revenue $1.66B. Data-center-specific revenue is missing.
customer_backlog_contract_segment_revenu.customer_concentration: Broad customer categories are disclosed, but data-center customer concentration is missing.

management_commentary_program_level_evid.summary: Management commentary supports increased electricity demand from AI/electrification and continued C&I opportunities in markets such as data centers. It is not enough to prove MYRG-specific AI data center revenue or backlog.

future_optionality.summary: Future optionality comes from AI-driven electrification and data-center construction increasing demand for electrical contracting and interconnection work. This is not current commercial validation unless tied to disclosed contracts, backlog, revenue, or customer commitments.
future_optionality.should_affect_rating: No upgrade beyond Watchlist based only on optionality.

bear_case.summary: MYRG may be a good electrical contractor rather than a true Serenity chokepoint. The data-center link is real but not quantified; contractor margins can be project-risk-sensitive; and price momentum indicates substantial repricing.
key_bear_points: No data-center-specific backlog, revenue, margin, or customer evidence.
Project execution risk: fixed-price contracts, labor/material costs, delays, change orders, and liquidated damages can impair margins.
Crowding risk is high after 172.8% 12m price performance and near-52-week-high trading.
A large portion of the thesis may be broader electrification/grid capex rather than AI data center-specific.

upgrade_triggers: Disclosure of data-center-specific backlog, contracts, or awards with project values.
Evidence that C&I data-center revenue is material and growing faster than the rest of C&I.
Segment margin or project margin evidence showing data-center/electrical infrastructure work is accretive.
Named hyperscaler, colocation, EPC, utility, or industrial customers tied to data-center power projects.
Valuation/crowding reset while commercial evidence improves.
downgrade_triggers: Evidence that data-center work is immaterial within C&I or T&D.
Backlog growth stalls or C&I backlog declines materially.
Gross margin deterioration from labor, project inefficiencies, unfavorable change orders, or fixed-price contract overruns.
Price momentum continues while commercial evidence remains unquantified.
Debt/cash flow worsens or FCF turns negative without backlog conversion.
final_rating_rationale: Keep MYRG as Watchlist. It has real electrical infrastructure relevance, explicit data-center contract-category evidence, strong backlog/RPO, positive FCF, and net cash. It does not reach High Priority Research because the data-center-specific customer, contract, backlog, revenue, and margin evidence is still missing and crowding risk is high.
